title: Beef Bourguignon
description: My Thermomix version of Julia Child's beef bourguignon — a rich red-wine beef stew, low and slow in one bowl.
intro: Julia made this French classic the dish everyone wants to master. The Thermomix simmers it low and slow on Reverse so the beef goes meltingly tender — no babysitting the pot.
steps: Place onion and garlic in the mixing bowl and chop 4 sec / 0°C / speed 5. Scrape down.
Add the olive oil and bacon and sauté 4 min / 120°C / speed 1.
Add the beef and cook 5 min / 120°C / Reverse / speed 1 to seal.
Add the flour and cook 1 min / 100°C / Reverse / speed 1 to coat.
Add the wine, stock, tomato paste, bay and thyme. Cook 60 min / 100°C / Reverse / speed 1, MC off and basket on the lid, adding the mushrooms and baby onions for the last 20 minutes.
Season and rest. Serve with mash or crusty bread; finish 1 min / 0°C / no speed.
tips: For deeper flavour, brown the beef in a hot pan first if you have time.

faq.q: Can the Thermomix make beef bourguignon?
faq.a: Yes — it sautés, seals and simmers low-and-slow on Reverse so the beef turns tender without stirring.
Chuck — its connective tissue melts during the slow cook.

faq.q: Is this the original Julia Child recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Julia Child and beef bourguignon; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.
